description: Microsoft FY2023 real-world generalization test — proves verifier works on a different company with different P&L label names
table_source: Microsoft FY2023 Annual Report — Consolidated Income Statement (In millions)

label_mapping_challenge.note: pnl_parser synonym matching must handle both without retraining

cases.question: What was Microsoft's revenue in FY2023?
cases.candidate_answer: Microsoft's revenue in FY2023 was 211,915 million.
cases.rationale: All claims independently verified against table data. ML P&L identity signal overridden: table may be structurally incomplete. No unsupported claims, no period or scale violations.
cases.notes: pnl_identity_fail_count=2 due to Microsoft table missing G&A expense row — post-ML ACCEPT gate overrides

cases.question: What was Microsoft's net income in FY2023?
cases.candidate_answer: Microsoft's net income in FY2023 was 72,361.
cases.rationale: All claims independently verified against table data. ML P&L identity signal overridden: table may be structurally incomplete. No unsupported claims, no period or scale violations.
cases.notes: Raw value 72,361 (no scale suffix) — grounding matches FY2023 net_income exactly

cases.question: What was Microsoft's gross margin in FY2023?
cases.candidate_answer: Microsoft's gross margin in FY2023 was 150,000 million.
cases.injected_error: Wrong value — actual gross margin was $135,620M
cases.rationale: ML decision: FLAG — unsupported claim value

cases.question: What was Microsoft's total revenue in FY2023?
cases.candidate_answer: Microsoft's total revenue in FY2023 was $211 billion.
cases.injected_error: Scale error — answer uses 'billion' but table is in millions
cases.rationale: P&L scale integrity violation — answer uses a different unit or scale than the evidence table.
cases.notes: Tier 1 scale label check fires independently of grounding — catches billion vs million even when numeric value is close

bugs_found_and_fixed.description: pnl_parser synonym collision: 'Sales and marketing' matched revenue via Pass-2 substring ('sales' in 'sales and marketing'), overwriting the actual Revenue row with $22,759M instead of $211,915M
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Added explicit exact synonyms to operating_expenses: 'sales and marketing', 'selling and marketing', 'marketing and sales', 'research and development', 'general and administrative'

bugs_found_and_fixed.description: Double scale normalization: LLM answer '211,915 million' has scale_token='million' so extractor already expanded to 211,915,000,000. execute_claim_against_table then multiplied again by scale_multiplier=1,000,000 → 2.12e17
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Added scale_token parameter to execute_claim_against_table; skip normalization when scale_token is not None

bugs_found_and_fixed.description: ML model predicted REPAIR/FLAG due to pnl_identity_fail_count=2 from incomplete Microsoft table (missing G&A expense row). Identity checks: operating_income = gross_margin - opex failed because only S&M was available as opex.
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Added post-ML ACCEPT gate in decision_model.decide(): when ML predicts REPAIR/FLAG but unsupported_claims_count=0 and coverage_ratio>=threshold and no hard violations, override to ACCEPT

different_from_apple: Label 'Cost of revenue' vs Apple's 'Total cost of sales'
Microsoft table missing G&A expense row (incomplete structure)
Microsoft uses raw values (no $ prefix in rows)
generalization_notes.pnl_parser_handled: All label differences resolved by synonym mapping without any Microsoft-specific configuration
